name: Monte Arabí Loop Trail
name_original: El Monte Arabí y la Cueva de la Horadada — Circular desde Pocico de la Buitrera
description: Explore the Monte Arabí Natural Monument on this 4.5-mile moderate hike to the unique Horadada Cave, starting from Pocico de la Buitrera.
region_intro: Hiking around Yecla offers diverse landscapes characterized by mountains, plains, and extensive vineyards. The region features unique natural monuments like Monte Arabí, known for its distinctive rock formations and archaeological significance. Hikers can explore trails through mountain ranges such as Sierra de Salinas and Sierra del Carche, which boast important forest ecosystems. The varied terrain includes steppes, ravines, and areas with natural springs, providing a range of environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Yecla The most popular hiking route is Monte Arabí Loop Trail, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route explores the unique rock formations of Monte Arabí and leads to the Horadada Cave. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cueva del Lagrimal – Sierra de Salinas loop from Colonias Sierra Salinas Yecla, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This trail features a significant elevation gain and traverses parts of the Sierra de Salinas mountain range. Local hikers also love the Piedra del Corazón – Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí loop from Monumento Natural Monte Arabí, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through the Monte Arabí Natural Monument, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Yecla is defined by mountains, steppes, and vineyards, with trails exploring unique rock formations and forest ecosystems. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging paths suitable for different ability levels. The routes in Yecla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Yecla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Yecla?
A: Yecla offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and a few more challenging routes to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Yecla?
A: Hiking in Yecla provides a rich tapestry of landscapes, from the distinctive rock formations of &lt;strong&gt;Monte Arabí&lt;/strong&gt; and the forested slopes of &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Salinas&lt;/strong&gt; to vast vineyards, almond groves, and open steppes. You'll encounter mountains, plains, ravines, and areas with natural springs, offering varied scenery throughout your hike.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Yecla?
A: Yes, Yecla has several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. Options like the 'Paseo entre Viñedos' offer pleasant walks through vineyards, and routes around 'Las Pansas' provide gentle terrain with great views. The 'Chicharra' Greenway, an old railway trackbed, is also a flat and accessible path ideal for relaxed walks.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes with significant elevation gain in Yecla?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Yecla offers routes with notable elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3957178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Lagrimal – Sierra de Salinas loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.3 km path with a significant elevation gain, traversing parts of the Sierra de Salinas mountain range. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of la Magfalena loop&lt;/a&gt;, which also features considerable ascent.

Q: What unique natural features can I explore on the trails around Yecla?
A: Yecla is home to several unique natural features. The 'mystical mountain' &lt;strong&gt;Monte Arabí&lt;/strong&gt; is a natural monument known for its distinctive rock formations and prehistoric rock art. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí&lt;/a&gt; and the intriguing bowl-like carvings in the rock surface. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Salinas&lt;/strong&gt; also offers important forest ecosystems and rewarding views from its summit.

Q: Are there any caves I can visit while hiking in Yecla?
A: Yes, Yecla features several interesting caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí&lt;/a&gt; is a large cavity showcasing the effects of wind erosion. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4264671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Lagrimal&lt;/a&gt; is another notable cave, known for water dripping from its ceiling. These are often accessible via hiking trails in their respective areas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Yecla?
A: Many of Yecla's trails are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897891275/monte-arabi-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Arabí Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the Horadada Cave, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedra del Corazón – Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí loop&lt;/a&gt;. The 'Lomas de los Castillarejos' and 'Around Monte Colorado' also offer moderate circular options.

Q: Where can I find the best viewpoints in Yecla?
A: For panoramic vistas, the trails around &lt;strong&gt;Monte Arabí&lt;/strong&gt; offer excellent views of the surrounding countryside. Additionally, the series of wooden walkways on the &lt;strong&gt;Castle Hill (Cerro del Castillo)&lt;/strong&gt; provide spectacular views over Yecla town and its environs, leading up to the sanctuary of the Virgen del Castillo.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Yecla?
A: The hiking routes in Yecla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from unique rock formations to extensive vineyards, and the opportunity to explore significant natural monuments like Monte Arabí. The well-maintained paths and varied difficulty levels also contribute to a positive experience.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Yecla?
A: Yes, for popular hiking areas like Monte Arabí, public infrastructures including picnic areas and parking are available to facilitate access to the trails. For other routes, roadside parking or designated areas near villages are often an option.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Yecla dog-friendly?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in natural areas like Yecla are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage, particularly in protected natural monuments or areas with sensitive wildlife, to ensure a pleasant and responsible hike with your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Yecla?
A: The best time for hiking in Yecla is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. Spring also brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful colors in the vineyards.
tips: Monte Arabí is an interesting mountain range that stands out in the Altiplano region (Murcia), about 17 kilometers northwest of Yecla.

It is located 18 km NW of the urban area, on Mount Arabí.
The space is included in the delimitation of the environment for the protection of Cantos de Visera I and II, Abrigos del Mediodía I and II and petroglyphs, on Monte Arabí.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Pocico de la Buitrera, which is one of the main access points to the Monte Arabí area. Parking is generally available at these access points, making it convenient to begin your hike.

faqs.question: How difficult is the El Monte Arabí and Cueva de la Horadada circular trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. It's suitable for families and children, though some variants might require a good physical condition. The route covers about 7.2 km with an elevation gain of around 267 meters.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich experience. You'll encounter unique geological formations, prehistoric cave paintings (Arte Levantino and Arte Esquemático), and remains of Bronze Age settlements. Key highlights include the impressive Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí with its distinctive hole in the ceiling, and panoramic vistas from the View of Monte Arabí.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by its distinctive karstic landscape, featuring numerous caves, galleries, and unique rock formations. You'll traverse pleasant paths, some clearly marked and others more natural, occasionally passing through dense pine forests.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While Monte Arabí is a natural area, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on the varied terrain. There are no specific prohibitions mentioned, but always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Monte Arabí Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: Monte Arabí is designated as a Natural Monument and its cave paintings are a UNESCO World Heritage site. Generally, there are no entrance fees for hiking the trails. However, specific restrictions may apply to accessing certain sensitive areas or during particular periods to protect bird species, so it's advisable to check local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Monte Arabí and Cueva de la Horadada trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this trail are during the spring and autumn months. The weather is typically milder and more pleasant, making for a more enjoyable experience. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, though often still suitable for hiking.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Nuevo autorizado Arabí, Camino de las Pinturas, and Sendero Norte del Arabí.

faqs.question: What makes Monte Arabí a special place to visit?
faqs.answer: Monte Arabí is exceptional due to its status as the first Natural Monument in the Murcia Region and a UNESCO World Heritage site for its prehistoric cave paintings. It's a rich archaeological and geological enclave, known for its unique rock formations, ancient settlements, and the natural wonder of the Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí. It's also known as 'the magic mountain' with local legends and tales.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this circular hike is approximately 2 hours and 15 minutes (8074 seconds). This can vary depending on your pace and how much time you spend exploring the various points of interest along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this trail?
faqs.answer: While generally moderate, some paths can be natural and uneven due to the karstic terrain. Always wear appropriate footwear, carry enough water, and be mindful of your surroundings, especially when exploring caves or rock formations. During certain periods, restrictions might apply to protect bird species, so be aware of any signage.
